title: MCP server registration manifest
description: Declares the Model Context Protocol servers Atelier should launch / connect to at session start. Atelier ships with an MCP client in atelier-core (via the rmcp crate); this manifest is how users plug in any MCP-compliant server.

name.description: Unique identifier for this server within the session. Surfaces in the trust-budget UI and the egress audit log.

transport.description: Transport. stdio launches the server as a subprocess; http/sse connect to a remote endpoint and count as egress (§12).

env.description: Environment variables for the subprocess (stdio only). Values support interpolation tokens `${env:NAME}`. `${keychain:NAME}` tokens are reserved and fail closed until OS keychain support lands. Subject to §12 redaction policy on values. v60.37 SCH-M4 — values capped at 4 KiB to bound launcher memory + audit-log bloat.

url.description: Endpoint URL for http/sse transport. v60.37 SCH-M6 — http(s) only + 2 KiB cap; a misconfigured registration (e.g. `file:///etc/passwd`) is rejected at schema time.

headers.description: HTTP headers for http/sse transport. Values support interpolation tokens `${env:NAME}`. `${keychain:NAME}` tokens are reserved and fail closed until OS keychain support lands. Plaintext literals are accepted but discouraged for anything resembling a secret. `Authorization` headers are redacted in the §12 egress audit log; interpolation is resolved at request time and never persisted. v60.37 SCH-M4 — values capped at 4 KiB.

side_effect_class.description: Default §8 trust-budget classification applied to all tools advertised by this server. Per-tool overrides allowed in the tool manifest.

allow_net.description: For stdio, permits outbound network from the subprocess sandbox. For http/sse, must be true because the transport definitionally performs network egress.

allowed_hosts.description: http/sse transport only: hostnames the launcher is allowed to dispatch to on this server's behalf. Default when omitted = [host(url)]. Any `call_tool` whose effective URL host does not match is refused with `McpLaunchError::HostNotAllowed` and a `blocked` audit row.

enabled.description: Set to false to keep the registration in the file but skip launching the server.
